title: Do you think we will see a NTV version of Q this year
display_title: Do you think we will see a NTV version of Q this year
wikitext:
[]: Do you think we will see a NTV version of Q this year?             Quote Post by Dima » Sat Mar 29, 2014 1:15 pm So this is something i am thinking every now and then when i am entering this forum. When will the NTV version of Q will be released. If we remember what happened with 1.0 and 2.0 this means that the NTV version should air this summer with a new FINAL preview. All the tv versions of the films in Japan aired 2 years after the theatrical release. But Khara after the release of Q took a generally different approach compared to the previous film. First they released the DVD/BD version earlier then usual and without anything new. Then we have the absence of CRC and unless i am mistaken Groundworks arrived late this time and in 2 parts (the one was released recently, the other has no date yet). So i started to thinking maybe Anno will decide to not release the NTV version this year. Someone could say they may not release a TV version this time but this is highly unlikely in my opinion. I really hope though that the NTV version will air this summer with a new FINAL preview. This will mean that we are one step close to the FINAL release. Anyone remembers if the Q preview that appeared in 2.0 NTV had a release date for the film?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Mar 29, 2014 3:13 pm Jo: Theatrical release date announced (on khara website): Feb 13, 2007 (200 days) Theatrical release date: Sept 1, 2007 First home media release date: April 25, 2008 (237 days) Groundwork: April 25, 2008 (237 days) CRC: May 16, 2008 (258 days) NTV broadcast: July 3, 2009 (671 days) (Preview for 2.0 at the end uses actual movie footage) Ha: Theatrical release date announced (on khara website): Feb 29, 2009 (118 days) Theatrical release date: June 27, 2009 First home media release date: May 25, 2010 (332 days) Groundwork: #1 May 31, 2011 (703 days). #2 Oct 31, 2011 (856 days) CRC: Sept 6, 2010 (436 days) NTV broadcast: August 26, 2011 (790 days) (15s preview shows extremely early version of Operation US, narrows down the Q release to "Fall 2012") Q: Theatrical release date announced (Eva Extra event): July 01, 2012 (139 days) Theatrical release date: Nov 17, 2012 First home media release date: April 24, 2013 (158 days) Groundwork: #1 March 14, 2014 (482 days). #2 August 22, 2014 (643 days) CRC: ??? NTV broadcast: September 5, 2014 (657 days) Honestly there really isn't enough to claim a pattern with any certainty... EDIT: updated. green stuff is new information since the original psot date, for future reference.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Sat Mar 29, 2014 3:52 pm Thanks a lot for posting this (you even have the dates!). Yeah it doesn't seem to be a pattern here except as i said in the OP that the NTV version for Jo and Ha released in summer and 2 years after the theatrical release. I think the month of the release doesn't matter as long as there is a 2 year space and it is summer. We will find soon enough....

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Mar 29, 2014 7:28 pm Love that TM keeps track of all the dates/days since! Have an Asuka for your helpful post: To prevent off-topic about the Asuka emoticon, please see and reply here .

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Jul 18, 2014 12:37 am Seems i was right about the TV version of Q this year [url]http://blog.esuteru.com/archives/7775224.html[/url] Sorry i can't translate the full article but pretty sure it means the TV version of Q will air September 9th.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jul 18, 2014 12:40 am Yep, it does. http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3126.html Also, Death and EoE will air on Japanese TV in August, but who cares about that. Hype for new preview at the end of the TV version. Exact lineup: August 18th: Death (true)2 August 25: EoE (Death and EoE are part of NTV's Movie Heaven block (on Monday(s?)), different from Friday Roadshow.) August 22nd: Eva 1.0 TV Version August 29th: Eva 2.0 TV Version September 5th: Eva 3.0 TV Version + God Warrior TV Version Edit: Slight clarification.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Jul 18, 2014 12:45 am I think it is the first time EoE will be aired alongside the Rebuild movies. Hope the new preview will be awesome

[]:             Quote Post by Adaminator1 » Fri Jul 18, 2014 12:57 am Does this mean definitively we'll get a new preview for Final on September 5? This is my first rodeo with being around for a new Rebuild film. And do you have links to the old previews?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jul 18, 2014 1:08 am In the past I told people to send me a PM, but I'll just post the MF links and hope they don't get removed by MF. At the end of 1.01-TV...2.0! http://www.mediafire.com/?t4levxup4uhmh5o At the end of 2.22-TV...3.0! http://www.mediafire.com/?mvss5xycvl82pc7 Wouldn't surprise me if these are included in some BD-boxset sometime down the line.

[]:             Quote Post by WunderBah » Fri Jul 18, 2014 1:13 am Can't wait for September then lol

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Jul 18, 2014 1:42 am Hmm... Airing ALL the Evangelion movies in such a short period of time does hint towards something worthwhile. Fingers crossed.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Jul 18, 2014 1:48 am This probably means FINAL will be released next year, unless they will pull a megaton with a 2014 release.

[]:             Quote Post by Adaminator1 » Fri Jul 18, 2014 1:54 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: In the past I told people to send me a PM, but I'll just post the MF links and hope they don't get removed by MF. At the end of 1.01-TV...2.0! http://www.mediafire.com/?t4levxup4uhmh5o At the end of 2.22-TV...3.0! http://www.mediafire.com/?mvss5xycvl82pc7 Wouldn't surprise me if these are included in some BD-boxset sometime down the line.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Jul 18, 2014 2:06 am I'm 100% certain it'll be next year. Not that it matters in the scheme of things & is all just convenient marketing but I can't imagine the creative team would want to miss out on 2015 since it'll be the 20th Anniversary of Evangelion. Hell, I'm expecting EvaGeeks to really be booming throughout all of 2015 as Gainax/Khara/Whoever finds various ways to shill, merchandise, materialize & profit off THE 20TH ANNIVERSARY OF EVANGELION. ... it also doesn't hurt that most of the original show TAKES PLACE in the year 2015 either.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jul 18, 2014 4:01 am A new preview? I don't know if I can handle this. My body is not ready. There's so much I have yet to complete. Things to write, discussions to have...

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jul 18, 2014 4:07 am A proper trailer for FINAL? ...be still my beating heart.

[]:             Quote Post by LightDragonman » Fri Jul 18, 2014 4:08 am About dang time eh? Let's just hope that FINAL won't pull a Matrix Revolutions on the saga, and leave us with more questions than answers.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Jul 18, 2014 4:11 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: A proper trailer for FINAL?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jul 18, 2014 4:13 am Proper trailer aside, I'll just be satisfied if we even get a hint of what's going to be in FINAL. Some new images, crappy storyboards... I mean, this is the first time that I've been involved in current Evangelion hype. I think I'm drooling a little on the inside. And maybe the outside too.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Jul 18, 2014 4:33 am There's also the question of whether the storyboards in Sagisu's Petit film have any relevance (I used an idea from them in my fanfic), and so whether we'll see anything perceptibly related to them.
